title: TMA-TNP  Section Map and Slide Processing - Phase 3
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b6mjb","text":"Human Tumor Atlas Tissue MicroArrary TNP  (TMA-TNP)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4a3ep","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"35sqg","text":"The Tissue MicroArray (TMA) TNP will extend the  SARDANA-TNP characterization and analytics methodologies for evaluation and validation on a large array of breast tumor samples that provide a broad spectrum of disease state and subtype. A commercially available anonymized breast tumor TMA was purchased and sections distributed to participating HTAN Centers. Some deidentified basic clinical data will also be provided. The participating HTAN Centers will characterize the FFPE specimens (e.g. by imaging) and generate a spatially resolved cell type/state census using each Center’s method of choice. Data will then be recorded in a common repository to enable joint analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"erklp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6rn3g","text":"This TNP has two specific Aims focused on (i) Data Collection and (ii) Data Coordination and Analysis. Individual Centers can participate in one or both aims.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4bbat","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2on1c","text":"The custom TMA design for this project was generated by Dr. Koei Chin (OHSU) and sent to Pantomics for custom TMA FFPE block generation and sectioning.  The TMA design and clinical sample descriptions can be found here:  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d28to","text":"dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bn2fmgbn .","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":41}],"data":{}},{"key":"16snk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9o9q0","text":"This protocol describes the procedure by which the OHSU OMS Atlas HTAN Center sectioned and distributed TMA sections for Phase 3 analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"204025CCB06211ECBCB00A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bn2fmgbn"}}}}
steps: Verify the identity of the FFPE block to be cut against written request for sectioning. The FFPE block (TMA1) will be utilized for TMA-TNP Phase 3.

Each slide was labeled with a unique OHSU Slide ID corresponding to the FFPE section map (below). A B C D E 1 Slide Label Name Slide # Description Institution Thickness (μm) 2 OHSU_TMA1_024 24 H&E OHSU 5 3 OHSU_TMA1_025 25 t-CycIF (Tumor Panel) HMS 5 4 OHSU_TMA1_026 26 t-CycIF (Immune Panel) HMS 5 5 OHSU_TMA1_027 27 mIHC (Discovery Panel) OHSU 5 6 OHSU_TMA1_028 28 cmIF (G3A Panel) Cyclic IF OHSU 5 7 OHSU_TMA1_029 29 cmIF (G3B Panel) Cyclic IF OHSU 5 8 OHSU_TMA1_030 30 GeoMx DSP Assay (Nanostring) protein ½ TMA (top 4 rows) OHSU 5 9 OHSU_TMA1_031 31 GeoMx DSP Assay (Nanostring) WTA ½ TMA (top 4 rows) OHSU 5 10 OHSU_TMA1_032 32 GeoMx DSP Assay (Nanostring) WTA ½ TMA (bottom 4 rows) OHSU 5 11 OHSU_TMA1_033 33 GeoMx DSP Assay (Nanostring) protein ½ TMA (bottom 4 rows) OHSU 5 12 OHSU_TMA1_034 34 H&E OHSU 5 13 OHSU_TMA1_035 35 extra - send to HMS for t-CycIF OHSU 5 14 OHSU_TMA1_036 36 extra OHSU 5 Slide ID Section # Description Institution Thickness (μm) OHSU_TMA1_000 0 QC H&E Pantomics OHSU 4 OHSU_TMA1_001 1 cmIF (G3B Panel) Cyclic Immunoflourescence OHSU 4 OHSU_TMA1_002 2 cmIF (G3A Panel ) Cyclic Immunoflourescence OHSU 4 OHSU_TMA1_003 3 H&E OHSU 4 OHSU_TMA1_004 4 t-CycIF (Tumor Panel) Cyclic Immunoflourescence HMS 4 OHSU_TMA1_005 5 t-CycIF (Immune Panel) Cyclic Immunoflourescence HMS 4 OHSU_TMA1_006 6 mIHC Multiplex IHC (Discovery Panel) OHSU 4 OHSU_TMA1_007 7 cmIF (G3A Panel) Cyclic Immunoflourescence OHSU 4 OHSU_TMA1_008 8 cmIF (G3B Panel) Cyclic Immunoflourescence OHSU 4 OHSU_TMA1_009 9 H&E OHSU 4 OHSU_TMA1_010 10 t-CycIF (Tumor Panel) Cyclic Immunoflourescence HMS 4 OHSU_TMA1_011 11 t-CycIF (Immune Panel) Cyclic Immunoflourescence HMS 4 OHSU_TMA1_012 12 mIHC Multiplex IHC (Discovery Panel) OHSU 4 OHSU_TMA1_013 13 cmIF (reserve) Cyclic Immunoflourescence OHSU 4 OHSU_TMA1_014 14 cmIF (reserve) Cyclic Immunoflourescence OHSU 4
Align block on microtome to minimize tissue loss.
Face into block at 5μm until full section of tissue is achieved.
Cut adequate ribbon at 5μm to cover all serial sections.

Mount tissue sections onto Tanner slides to maintain serial order and orientation of sections. Sections for Nanostring must be placed in a shifted orientation to capture the cores of interest (2 sections/slides to be combined for full representation of TMA1).
All Slides are immediately baked in a 45 °C oven (~45-48C) for 2 hours prior to distribution.

In the lab, the slides are baked in an oven at 55 °C Overnight, (12-16 hours) (except for #30-33 for Nanostring) , and then at 65 °C for 00:30:00, (30-45 min) Note: Slides should be baked at 65 °C for at least 30 minutes. The slides are stored in a 4 °C cold room until deparaffinization or shipping.
Slides were shipped on 4 °C ice packs by express shipping per the FFPE spatial map noted above.
creator: Heidi S Feiler
